title: Nuclear icebreakers & submarines: How Russia, China aim to shatter US hold on global trade
content: The Arctic is emerging as a critical new maritime highway, driven by Russia and China's strategic investments in infrastructure and icebreaker fleets. This development offers shorter trade routes between Asia and Europe, challenging traditional chokepoints and reshaping global power dynamics as the US plays catch-up.

title: India attends UK-led talks on Strait of Hormuz opening; MEA says 'in touch with Iran'
content: India is set to join the UK-led discussions focusing on reopening the vital Strait of Hormuz, as confirmed by the Ministry of External Affairs. The Foreign Secretary is expected to represent India at this pivotal meeting.

title: 'When you were in caves ...': Iran taunts US over 'Stone Age' threat
content: Iran has sharply retorted to US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's "stone age" threats, highlighting Iran's ancient civilization and resilience. The Iranian embassy in South Africa countered that while Americans were "still in caves," Iran was inscribing human rights.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook finds Trump administration is very accessible: What he said
content: Apple CEO Tim Cook finds the Trump administration "very accessible," emphasizing the importance of dialogue over sideline criticism.  He believes engaging with those in power, even with differing views, is crucial for navigating complex global issues and fostering understanding in a polarized world. Cook maintains Apple's core values remain steadfast.

title: 'This isn't a show!' Macron ridicules Trump after US prez mocks his marriage
content: French President Macron has sharply criticised Donald Trump's remarks about his marriage, deeming them "neither elegant nor up to standard." Trump's comments, made during a speech and a video, also targeted Nato allies. French political figures across the spectrum have condemned Trump's personal attacks, emphasizing the gravity of global issues.

title: Mustafizur row: IPL chief Dhumal breaks silence - 'Govt is supportive of ...'
content: IPL chairman Arun Singh Dhumal called Mustafizur Rahman’s release from Kolkata Knight Riders “unfortunate” after BCCI instructed the move, sparking controversy. The issue had wider fallout, with Bangladesh later replaced by Scotland in the 2026 T20 World Cup. Dhumal said the government has no role in daily cricket matters, while KKR confirmed they followed BCCI directives.

title: Oracle layoffs: 6 AM email that laid off 10,000+ employees did not come from HR
content: Oracle has begun its largest-ever layoffs, impacting thousands globally without prior notice. Employees received termination emails early Tuesday, with immediate system access revocation.  The company's aggressive AI data center expansion is cited as a key driver for these cuts, which could affect up to 30,000 staff. Severance packages require signing documents first.

title: 'Trump controls PM': Rahul Gandhi attacks Modi in Assam, calls Himanta 'most corrupt CM'
content: Rahul Gandhi launched a scathing attack on PM Modi and Assam CM Sarma, alleging foreign influence and corruption ahead of the 2026 elections. Gandhi claimed Trump controls Modi and accused Sarma of running a "land ATM" with Modi and Shah. Congress also unveiled poll promises including financial aid for women and land rights.

title: 'Contradicting himself every day': Macron mocks Trump, rejects US military plan for Hormuz
content: French president Macron criticized Donald Trump's inconsistent Iran policy, deeming military action to reopen the Strait of Hormuz unrealistic. Macron emphasized the need for de-escalation and renewed negotiations. Trump's shifting stances on allies, escalation, and war objectives have drawn international and domestic criticism, with many nations hesitant to join US efforts.

title: Ranjith Balakrishnan arrest: WCC questions Kerala safety over assault allegations
content: Malayalam filmmaker Ranjith Balakrishnan faces arrest following a sexual assault complaint by an actress. Allegedly, he entered her caravan without consent on a film set.  The Women in Cinema Collective has voiced strong criticism, questioning systemic failures in protecting women. Ranjith denies the charges, asserting his innocence and preparing a bail plea.

title: Corporate recruiter on what laid off staff of Amazon, Meta and Oracle failed to realise
content: Corporate recruiter Shreya Mehta issued a stark warning following Oracle's recent job cuts, emphasizing that loyalty and performance offer no guarantee of job security.  She advises employees to proactively update their resumes, maintain active professional networks, and cultivate adaptability, as real security lies in the ability to secure roles across various companies and industries, not just within a single employer.

title: 'For the IPL to move from 74 to 94 matches, we need a bigger window': IPL chairman Arun Dhumal
content: IPL Chairman Arun Singh Dhumal revealed expansion plans for the tournament, aiming for 84 or 94 matches. However, a significant hurdle is the crowded international cricket calendar. Dhumal suggested a potential shift away from bilateral series could create the necessary space for the IPL's growth beyond its current 74-game format.

title: 'For a very important bill': Parliament to reconvene on April 16; what’s on cards
content: Parliament is set to reconvene on April 16 to consider legislation that could increase Lok Sabha seats from 543 to 816, with 273 seats reserved for women. This move aims to implement a one-third reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies, significantly altering the country's political landscape and reinforcing the government's women's empowerment agenda.

title: Raghav Chadha sidelined? AAP's Rajya Sabha shuffle triggers rumours; what went wrong
content: Raghav Chadha has been removed as the Aam Aadmi Party's deputy leader in the Rajya Sabha. Ashok Mittal has been appointed in his place. The party has also requested the Rajya Sabha secretariat not to allot speaking time to Chadha from its quota. This move comes amid reports of indiscipline and not adhering to the party line.

title: Martina Navratilova warns Emma Raducanu after Miami Open withdrawal
content: Emma Raducanu’s coaching changes are back in focus after Martina Navratilova urged her to stay with one coach longer. Raducanu, however, prefers flexibility and wants to rediscover her natural game. With mixed results, recent injuries, and no full-time coach, she stands at a key moment. Her next decision could define how her career moves forward.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry team up with $150bn mogul
content: Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry have joined Warren Buffett in reviving his iconic charity lunch through a new auction titled “A Seat At The Table.” The initiative blends Buffett’s legacy in philanthropy with the Currys’ growing influence beyond basketball. With proceeds benefiting the Glide Foundation and Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation, the event aims to create meaningful change in underserved communities. Scheduled for June 24 in Omaha, the auction reflects a powerful collaboration focused on impact, purpose, and long-term community support.

title: Celtics vs Heat injury update: Who's playing, injured, team stats, and more
content: The Boston Celtics, leading their division, play the Miami Heat at Kaseya Center. Miami needs this win to secure a play-in spot. Both teams have injury concerns. Boston has dominated the season series. The Celtics are favored to win, but Miami's home advantage and determination could make it a close contest.

title: Trolled for documentary after 6 off Bumrah, Pakistan batter breaks Virat Kohli record
content: Sahibzada Farhan smashed an unbeaten 106 to guide Multan Sultans to a thrilling six-wicket victory over Hyderabad Kingsmen in the PSL. Chasing 226, Farhan's sensational century, supported by Steven Smith's quickfire 46, secured the fifth-highest successful chase in PSL history. This remarkable innings marked Farhan's ninth T20 century, placing him joint-fourth on the all-time list.

title: Delhi HC reserve verdict in Rajpal Yadav's debt case, actor makes emotional plea
content: Actor Rajpal Yadav's legal battle over a Rs 9 crore cheque bounce case saw the Delhi High Court reserve its verdict after settlement talks failed. Despite judicial intervention and an emotional plea from Yadav, who cited financial hardship, no agreement was reached.

title: 'Boring singer': Why Trump is gunning for 'The Boss'
content: President Donald Trump publicly criticized Bruce Springsteen on social media, accusing the musician of "Trump Derangement Syndrome" and calling him a "boring singer." Trump urged his MAGA supporters to boycott Springsteen's concerts, citing the singer's long-standing vocal opposition to his presidency and policies. This latest exchange continues a feud that began in 2016.

title: 15 years of Game of Thrones: Daenerys, Jon return in new ‘Reign of Thrones’ trailer
content: Fifteen years after its debut, the 'Game of Thrones' legacy is celebrated with a new trailer blending original moments and spin-off footage. Despite a divisive finale, HBO's expanding universe, including 'House of the Dragon' and upcoming prequels like 'A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms,' continues to captivate fans, promising more Westeros adventures.

title: 'Publicly berating captains”: Lalit Modi’s jibe at Goenka after LSG match
content: IPL founder Lalit Modi criticized franchise owners for public displays of anger after losses, contrasting them with football league professionalism. He questioned their focus on hype over long-term vision, infrastructure, and fan experience, urging a more patient and professional approach to enhance the league's value.

title: 'Are we Mossad agents in Iran?': World No. 2 USA Nakamura blasts FIDE
content: World No. 2 American grandmaster Hikaru Nakamura criticised FIDE’s anti-cheating measures at the 2026 Candidates in Cyprus, calling them “complete nonsense.” He joked, “Are we like Mossad agents inside Iran or something?” Nakamura hasn’t raised concerns with FIDE, saying the fears are overblown. After four rounds, he has 1.5 points, sharing results with Anish Giri, Andrey Esipenko, and Wei Yi.

title: When will Artemis 2 reach the Moon? All you need to know about historic mission
content: In a monumental leap for space exploration, NASA's Artemis II mission is set to reignite humanity's lunar legacy, sending a crew of four astronauts on a groundbreaking 10-day odyssey to the Moon.

title: ISL: AIFF granted Mohun Bagan, Kerala 3-day extension to pay participation fee
content: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) has granted Mohun Bagan Super Giant and Kerala Blasters an extension until April 5 to pay their first ISL participation fee. Failure to remit the full amount, including daily fines, could lead to disqualification from the next ISL season.

title: Maldives, Sri Lanka others turn to India for energy security amid crisis
content: Amidst escalating Middle East conflict, India is providing petroleum supplies to neighboring nations like Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, Nepal, and Bhutan. The Maldives has also requested petroleum products, with India examining these requests based on its own availability.

title: 'Bengal welcomes tourists': TMC taunts Shah with fish, meat menu ahead of 15-day visit
content: The Trinamool Congress has sharpened its attack on Union Home Minister Amit Shah ahead of his visit to West Bengal, recommending local non-vegetarian delicacies. This move aligns with the TMC's campaign, which portrays the BJP as out of touch with Bengal's cultural traditions, particularly regarding food habits.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 15: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 950 crore India net
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has made box office history, officially crossing the massive Rs. 900 crore India net milestone in just 14 days. The Aditya Dhar directorial, starring Ranveer Singh as the lethal spy Hamza, has collected a staggering Rs 922.26 crore domestic net so far. Despite the ongoing IPL 2026 season, the film continues to draw huge crowds, even outperforming the lifetime collections of the first 'Dhurandhar' in just 11 days. With a global haul of Rs' 1,466 crore, this action epic featuring Sanjay Dutt and Arjun Rampal is now rapidly chasing the elite Rs' 1,000 crore India club.

title: AAP removes Raghav Chadha as party's deputy leader in Rajya Sabha
content: The Aam Aadmi Party has requested the Rajya Sabha Secretariat to remove Raghav Chadha as Deputy Leader of the party in the Upper House.  The party also wants him excluded from speaking time allocated to AAP, proposing Ashok Mittal for the Deputy Leader role. This move indicates a significant internal leadership adjustment in Parliament, though official reasons remain undisclosed.

title: IPL: After snub, RCB star heads to court to secure playing rights
content: Sri Lankan fast bowler Nuwan Thushara has taken Sri Lanka Cricket (SLC) to court, seeking a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for the 2026 Indian Premier League. Thushara argues that SLC's refusal, citing fitness standards, is unreasonable as his contract ended and he informed them of his departure from international cricket.

title: Kylian Mbappe reacts as LEGO unveils Cristiano Ronaldo, Lionel Messi World Cup 2026 set
content: LEGO has launched a new football-themed collection. The collaboration has been launched to celebrate the upcoming FIFA World Cup 2026. The collection features the top names in football. It includes Cristiano Ronaldo, Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, and Vinícius Júnior. Each player has been turned into a LEGO minifigure, giving fans a fun and creative way to celebrate their favorite stars.

title: Oil prices soar, crypto sinks, stock markets crash: Market reaction to Trump speech on Iran
content: US President Trump's conflicting statements on the Iran conflict, suggesting both military success and potential escalation, have triggered global market volatility. Indian stocks initially plunged but recovered, while oil prices surged, impacting nations like Nepal and Bhutan. Precious metals and cryptocurrencies also saw significant declines amid heightened uncertainty.

title: Candidates Chess: How risk-averse Indians self-sabotaged in Round 4
content: India's chess contingent faced a disappointing Round 4 at the Candidates tournament, with R Praggnanandhaa and R Vaishali securing draws while Divya Deshmukh suffered a significant loss. Praggnanandhaa's game was described as uncharacteristically subdued, and Vaishali narrowly escaped a difficult position. Deshmukh's experimental play led to fundamental errors and a decisive defeat.

title: BJP alleges ‘lawlessness’ in West Bengal over Malda incident, calls for Mamata’s resignation
content: Party spokesperson Gaurav Bhatia said ‘jungle raj’ was prevailing in the State under Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Gherao of judicial officers is a challenge to Supreme Court’s authority: CJI
content: Top court says the incident was not routine but was a ‘calculated, well-planned and deliberate move’ to demoralise judicial officers and affect the adjudication process; CJI says never seen ‘such a politically polarised State’

title: Polavaram Hydro Electric Project works gather pace
content: AP-Genco plans to commission three out of its 12 units of 80 MW capacity each by July 2027 and complete the project in all respects by January 2028

title: People have understood the reality of the BJP, claims Siddaramaiah
content: The Chief Minister campaigns for the party candidate Samarth for the second day in Davangere South Assembly Constituency

title: T.N. Assembly election: Udhayanidhi files nomination
content: The deputy chief minister has declared movable assets to the tune of ₹12.92 crore and immovable assets of ₹7.72 crore

title: All-time high passenger revenue of ₹2,420 crore recorded by Bengaluru railway division in 2025–26
content: Divisional Railway Manager Ashutosh Kumar Singh, addressing a press conference at the divisional office on Wednesday, said that the division witnessed robust growth across sectors including freight, parcel, catering and infrastructure development.

title: Kerala Assembly polls: M.A. Baby launches sharp attack on Congress, defends LDF’s record
content: CPI(M) general secretary accuses UDF of hypocrisy, challenges Congress’s moral standing in making allegations of political deals. He also questions the transparency of its fundraising efforts in Wayanad for rehabilitation.

title: Delimitation: Vizag wards rise to 120, Vijayawada to have 86, Tirupati 66
content: Guntur, Nellore, Kurnool, Anantapur and other Urban Local Bodies in Andhra Pradesh also see significant rise in the number of wards based on population, as per MAUD Department’s circular for delimitation

title: Kerala polls: LDF, UDF make grandiose plans for  health sector
content: LDF promises to continue the work it has been doing the past 10 years, with better focus on many key sectors such as elderly care, chronic diseases, epidemic management, and food safety.  UDF promises to increase  Budget allocation for health and make  dedicated provisions for medicines and medical investigations, two components which significantly contribute to the out-of-pocket expenditure on health

title: Roundtable demands allocation of 50% share of KG Basin gas to Andhra Pradesh
content: Citing the recommendations of the 12th Finance Commission, the participants urged Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu to press the Centre for the State’s rightful share

title: Assembly Election LIVE: ECI appoints Sandeep Rai Rathore to head Tamil Nadu police
content: The ECI also appointed Additional Director General of Police Sanjay Kumar, a 1997-batch IPS officer, as the Commissioner of Police, Tambaram and Abhishek Dixit as the Commissioner of Police, Madurai

title: Theatre veteran Prasanna rejects State aid, slams govt.’s apathy towards theatre
content: The government has sanctioned ₹75,000 to our institute, which is four times less than what some amateur groups have received. I respectfully reject this aid, he says

title: Odisha unit of BJP throws its weight behind party’s campaign in West Bengal elections
content: Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi is likely to make eight to ten visits to West Bengal for campaign

title: Confusion at Mee Seva centres as fee hike kicks in, clarity yet to reach the ground
content: The government has announced a hike of over 77% in service charges effective April 1, most centres have yet to display updated price charts

title: CPI hails Amaravati as rightful capital of Andhra Pradesh, faults YSRCP’s stance
content: The party also criticises Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu for not taking up the Special Category Status issue along with the Amaravati Bill

title: ‘Operation Chabutra’ sparks debate over safety and freedom on Hyderabad streets
content: Operation Chabutra draws mixed public response, with views differing on its impact on safety, policing methods and freedom of movement in Hyderabad

title: Karnataka High Court declines to quash 11 FIRs in ₹40-crore loan fraud case
content: The FIRs were registered on the complaints lodged by businessmen and professionals from Mumbai, Hyderabad, Lucknow, Bengaluru, and Kerala

title: Andhra Pradesh seeks higher Bengal gram procurement under Price Support Scheme
content: It wants the Centre to increase the procurement limit from 94,500 MT to 4,57,365 MT, in line with revised production estimates

title: Reconstruction of Polavaram diaphragm wall completed, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: It had to be rebuilt at an additional cost of ₹1,000 crore due to damage caused during the previous regime, he says, adding that work on the main structure, the Earth-Cum-Rock Fill dam, will now gain momentum

title: Trump ‘controlling’ Modi, claims Rahul Gandhi in Assam election rally
content: Mr. Modi handed energy security over to the U.S. to an extent that India cannot buy oil from Russia, Iran, and Iraq without seeking Mr. Trump’s permission, alleges Congress leader; he calls BJP leader Himanta Biswa Sarma the most corrupt Chief Minister of the country

title: Speaker flags Manjhi’s absence during Question Hour, cautions MPs and Ministers against long chats in House
content: Manjhi was absent from the House during Question Hour when a query related to the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Ministry was taken up

title: Relief to govt. staff, Naidu gives nod to clear ₹7,358 crore pending dues
content: PF dues of ₹1,848 crore directly credited into employees’ accounts, clearing of ₹3,411 crore in gratuity dues and ₹1,800 crore towards leave encashment for retired employees is in the process, say officials; Naidu directs payments to small contractors on priority basis

title: Nandyal MP seeks UNESCO’s Intangible Cultural Heritage tag for Ahobilam Paruveta Utsavam
content: Tribals of 33 villages in Nandyal district have been celebrating Paruvata Utsavam for 40 days every year for the past several decades, says Shabari

title: Foreign Secretary Misri represents India at U.K.-hosted meet on Strait of Hormuz
content: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, joining the deliberations through virtual mode, articulated India's stance on safety of international shipping lanes in the region

title: Naidu and Pawan hail passage of ‘Amaravati Bill’ in both Houses of Parliament
content: The aspirations of the people have found a resounding voice at the national level, says the Chief Minister, while the Deputy Chief Minister observes that justice has finally been delivered to the people of the State

title: ECI appoints Sandeep Rai Rathore to head Tamil Nadu police; new Commissioners of Police for Tambaram, Madurai
content: Sanjay Kumar posted as Commissioner of Police, Tambaram, and Abhishek Dixit was posted as Commissioner of Police, Madurai; R.V. Ramya Bharti posted as Inspector-General of Police, West Zone

title: Three-round NFL mock draft: Kiper, Miller, Reid and Yates rotate through the top 100 picks
content: We asked four draft analysts -- Mel Kiper Jr., Matt Miller, Jordan Reid and Field Yates -- to make picks in the first three rounds. Where do the top prospects land?

title: These flaws could hold back the draft's top QBs
content: We saw a lot during the college careers of the draft's top QBs. Each has something that could set them back.

title: Arrr you ready: Pirates call up teen phenom Griffin
content: The Pirates are calling up 19-year-old shortstop Konnor Griffin, the No. 1 prospect in baseball, ahead of Friday's home opener against the Orioles.

title: Big 12's Yormark, TT booster bicker over schedule
content: Big 12 commissioner Brett Yormark and Texas Tech booster Cody Campbell traded barbs this week over a scheduled Friday night game between the Red Raiders and Houston next season.

title: Iowa State hoops star Crooks entering portal
content: Iowa State women's basketball star Audi Crooks will be entering the transfer portal for her final year of collegiate eligibility, she announced on social media Thursday.

title: Fanatics takes over exclusive NFL card license
content: A 20-year deal between Fanatics and the NFL has kicked in, consolidating the league's and players' union's trading card licenses under one company.

title: Rams receiver Nacua is in rehab, attorney says
content: Rams wide receiver Puka Nacua has checked in to rehab, his attorney told The California Post. Last month, a woman in Los Angeles filed a lawsuit against Nacua alleging he made an "unprovoked antisemitic statement" and later bit her on the shoulder.

title: Wemby stays hot, doubles down on MVP chase
content: After putting up 41 points and 18 rebounds in a runaway win, Spurs center Victor Wembanyama reiterated that the MVP award is squarely in his sights.

title: NCAA proposes ban on players who stay in draft
content: The NCAA Division I Cabinet will consider proposed changes to eligibility rules, including one that would bar athletes who have entered and remained in a professional sports draft from competing in college.

title: ⚾ MLB Power Rankings: Top teams after week 1
content: With baseball now underway, how much has changed since our preseason rankings? Our Week 1 rankings tell the story.

title: The latest on Kawhi Leonard, Steve Ballmer and the NBA's investigation into the LA Clippers
content: What was actually in Leonard's contract? What do league insiders think of it? Here's what we know about the allegations -- and where the investigation stands.

title: 'Nobody would've believed it if there wasn't a video': Remembering Randy Johnson's bird pitch
content: In 2001, one of baseball's first viral moments played out on a spring training field. Those who were there relive the "Sea of Feathers" game.

title: Ranking the top 55 WNBA free agents, from MVPs to key rotation players
content: More than 100 players are WNBA free agents. We break down what factors are at play in the decision-making process.

title: Your men's Final Four guide: Illinois-UConn, Arizona-Michigan (with predictions!)
content: We run through every team's keys to advancing to the championship game -- and their potential fatal flaws.

title: Chasing history, Arizona is staying present in run to Final Four
content: The Wildcats are back in the national semifinals for the first time in 25 years -- but are focused on the moment.

title: Who will go No. 1 in June's NBA draft? Where could Boozer land? We asked execs
content: Fit over talent? Versatility over production? NBA scouts and execs break down the conversations happening at the top of the draft.

title: No seats, no silence: A night inside the Clippers' Wall
content: Built to disrupt, the Intuit Dome's most intense section affects games in real time by turning fans into participants -- and opponents into targets.

title: How Brandon Bussi's brother, Dylan, continues to inspire his NHL journey
content: The 27-year-old goalie has had a record-breaking debut season for the Hurricanes, and his brother remains in his mindset.

title: NHL playoff watch: Clinching day for the Sabres, Hurricanes, Wild?
content: Buffalo, Carolina and Minnesota can punch playoff tickets on Thursday. Here's what to watch in a 14-game slate.

title: NBA playoff watch: MVP showdown in OKC?
content: Luka and SGA go head-to-head. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Wednesday.

title: USMNT big board: Did March losses finalize Pochettino's squad?
content: The March international window was the last time for players to impress U.S. coach Mauricio Pochettino, so who cemented their place on his team?

title: Kit ranking: Assessing every jersey we'll see this summer
content: The World Cup will be the first with 48 teams, which means 96 kits! Here is every one, from worst to first.

title: Who are the best 21 women's players aged 21 or under in the world?
content: Welcome to the next generation of women's stars aged 21 or under, with representatives from the NWSL, WSL and across Europe.

title: Inside the friction between Jalen Hurts and the Eagles
content: ESPN pulls back the curtain on what's ailing a star-studded Eagles offense, one in which its QB must evolve.

content: The challenge system leaderboard ranks batters, pitchers, catchers, teams and umps throughout the season.

content: Who are the favorites to win seasonlong honors? Here are the odds for MVP, Cy Young and Rookie of the Year in the AL and NL.

title: The new aces? Five pitchers poised for a breakout season
content: Tristan H. Cockcroft takes a look at five pitchers who are likely to be on many a fantasy championship roster.

title: Why new rules have Max Verstappen on the brink of walking away from F1
content: Max Verstappen predicted F1's new rule set would underwhelm, and 2026 is proving him correct, so much so that he might just quit the sport entirely.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal, Madrid eye young Leverkusen striker
content: Arsenal and Real Madrid are keen on Bayer Leverkusen striker Christian Kofane. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Bypass Hormuz With Nuclear Explosives? US Studied That In Panama In 1960s
content: The idea for a new canal to move oil from the Middle East had emerged two decades earlier, in the context of another Middle East conflict, the Suez crisis.

title: Bull Charges At Gujarat Man, Continues Attack Till Locals Step In For Rescue
content: Locals nearby were seen rushing to his rescue, throwing stones and attempting to ward away the bull by hitting it with sticks.

title: Newly Married Hyderabad Techie Dies By Suicide Over Husband's Dowry Torture
content: Ishita Yadav, was found hanging from the ceiling fan in the hall of her apartment on Wednesday evening. Her husband, Neeraj Bhansal, alerted the police after finding her.

title: India To Join 35 Countries' Effort To Reopen Strait Of Hormuz: Centre
content: The Strait of Hormuz is critical to the world economy because 20% of global energy supplies pass through the narrow waterway.

title: Indian Student Enrolments In US Decline By Nearly 7%: Centre Informs Rajya Sabha
content: On June 18, 2025, the US Department of State issued a press release titled, 'Announcement of Expanded Screening and Vetting for Visa Applicants'.

title: India's Defence Exports Hit Record Rs 38,424 Crore In 2025-26: Rajnath Singh
content: India's defence exports in 2024-25 was Rs 23,622 crore in 2024-25. It was Rs 21,083 crore in 2023-24.

content: Earlier, Prime Minister Narendra Modi held discussions with global leaders to address key international developments, including the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

content: The statement came from Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal during a briefing in the national capital on Thursday.

title: Delhi To Launch Real-Time Yamuna Water Quality Tracking From May
content: The monitors will be installed across six sites - Palla, ISBT Bridge, ITO, Nizamuddin Bridge, Okhla Barrage and Asgarpur.

title: As Artemis Roared Skyward, Shubhanshu Shukla Relives His Own Lift Off Moment
content: Artemis II set sail from the same Florida launch site that sent Apollo's explorers to the moon so long ago.

title: "Bid To Silence," Say Sources As AAP Demotes Raghav Chadha In Parliament
content: Ashok Mittal, who replaces Chadha as AAP's deputy leader in the Upper House of Parliament, is the chancellor of Lovely Professional University

content: In a video conference with commanders and the headquarters of the army, Commander-in-Chief of the Iranian army, Major General Amir Hatami, ordered the monitoring of "enemy movements with utmost pessimism and accuracy."

title: Multi-State Cyber Fraud Network Busted In Hyderabad, 19 Arrested In A Week
content: Investigators said the gang members operated from multiple states using WhatsApp groups, fake websites, mule bank accounts and mobile numbers obtained with forged documents.

content: Nikhitha M U was pursuing her final year of Bachelor of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery (BAMS) at Raghavendra Ayurvedic College in Karnataka's Malladihalli.

content: According to the family, the doctor was not present at the time of delivery, and the procedure was carried out by a staffer.

content: In the Gulf today, the oil taps cannot simply be turned back on. This is the distinction from the 1971 that not many are talking about.

content: Across regions, Gen Z voters share clear demands: less dynasty politics, better infrastructure, economic growth and stronger safety for women.

title: Why Big Ten dominance might be here to stay
content: coach dusty May asked how recent rule changes in college athletics helped league teams have such success . this narrative ignores many past scandals in the league, let alone that the Big Ten has produced plenty of contenders through the years . the overall sentiment is somewhat fair, however, though .

title: Trump’s Iran address: Peace is close, war is closer
content: the speech, delivered from the white house on Wednesday night, marked Trump’s most comprehensive attempt to define the trajectory of a conflict that has escalated rapidly over the past month . but rather than offering clarity, it raised fresh questions about the risk of further escalation, and the durability of Washington’s alliances and partnerships .
